tables.name: album
tables.description: Music albums (records, releases). Each album belongs to one artist and contains multiple tracks.

columns.name: album_id
columns.description: The column "album_id" represents a unique identifier for each album in the database.

columns.name: title
columns.description: The "title" column represents the names of music albums.

columns.name: artist_id
columns.description: The artist_id column represents a unique identifier for each artist associated with an album in the database.

tables.name: artist
tables.description: Musical artists, bands, and performers in the catalog.

columns.name: artist_id
columns.description: The column "artist_id" represents a unique identifier for each artist in the database.

columns.name: name
columns.description: The "name" column represents the names of musical artists or bands.

tables.name: customer
tables.description: Customers (buyers, accounts) who purchase music. Includes contact info, address, country. Can be assigned to a support rep employee.

columns.name: customer_id
columns.description: The "customer_id" column represents a unique identifier assigned to each customer in the database.

columns.name: first_name
columns.description: The "first_name" column represents the given names of customers, allowing for variable-length character entries up to 40 characters.

columns.name: last_name
columns.description: The "last_name" column represents the family or surname of customers, allowing for a maximum of 20 characters.

columns.name: company
columns.description: The "company" column represents the names of businesses or organizations associated with each customer.
examples: Embraer - Empresa Brasileira de Aeronáutica S.A.

columns.name: address
columns.description: The "address" column represents the residential or business locations of customers, formatted as variable-length strings.
examples: Rua dos Campeões Europeus de Viena, 4350

columns.name: city
columns.description: The "city" column represents the names of cities where customers reside.

columns.name: state
columns.description: The "state" column represents the abbreviated designations of geographical regions or states associated with customers.

columns.name: country
columns.description: The "country" column represents the country of residence for each customer, storing names of countries as variable-length strings.

columns.name: postal_code
columns.description: The "postal_code" column represents the mailing codes used to identify specific geographic areas for customer addresses, accommodating various formats including alphanumeric and numeric codes.

columns.name: phone
columns.description: The "phone" column represents the contact telephone numbers of customers, formatted to include international dialing codes and varying local formats.

columns.name: fax
columns.description: The "fax" column represents the fax numbers of customers, formatted with international dialing codes and varying styles.

columns.name: email
columns.description: The "email" column represents the email addresses of customers, used for communication and account identification.

columns.name: support_rep_id
columns.description: The `support_rep_id` column represents the unique identifier for the customer support representative assigned to assist each customer.
columns.business_rules: Internal customers have support_rep_id IS NOT NULL

tables.name: employee
tables.description: Company employees (staff). Some are support reps (agents) assigned to customers. Includes hire date, title, manager (reports_to).

columns.name: employee_id
columns.description: The "employee_id" column represents a unique identifier assigned to each employee in the database.

columns.name: last_name
columns.description: The "last_name" column represents the family names of employees in the organization.

columns.name: first_name
columns.description: The "first_name" column represents the given names of employees, limited to a maximum of 20 characters.

columns.name: title
columns.description: The "title" column represents the job positions or roles held by employees within the organization.

columns.name: reports_to
columns.description: The "reports_to" column represents the employee ID of the supervisor or manager to whom the employee directly reports.

columns.name: birth_date
columns.description: The "birth_date" column represents the date and time of birth for each employee, stored as a timestamp without time zone information.

columns.name: hire_date
columns.description: The "hire_date" column represents the date and time when each employee was hired, recorded as a timestamp without time zone information.

columns.name: address
columns.description: The "address" column represents the residential or work location of employees, formatted as variable-length strings containing street addresses.

columns.name: city
columns.description: The "city" column represents the names of cities where employees are located or reside.

columns.name: state
columns.description: The "state" column represents the abbreviation of the U.S. state where the employee is located or registered, as indicated by the sample value 'AB'.

columns.name: country
columns.description: The "country" column represents the country of residence or employment for each employee, stored as a variable-length string.

columns.name: postal_code
columns.description: The "postal_code" column represents the postal codes of employees' addresses, formatted according to Canadian postal code standards.

columns.name: phone
columns.description: The "phone" column represents the contact telephone numbers of employees, formatted to include country codes and area codes.

columns.name: fax
columns.description: The "fax" column represents the fax numbers of employees, formatted as strings that may include country codes and area codes.

columns.name: email
columns.description: The "email" column represents the email addresses of employees within the organization.

tables.name: genre
tables.description: Music genre classifications (Rock, Jazz, Classical, etc.).

columns.name: genre_id
columns.description: The column "genre_id" represents a unique identifier for each genre in the database.

columns.name: name
columns.description: The "name" column represents the various genres of music and entertainment, categorizing them by their specific styles or themes.

tables.name: invoice
tables.description: Sales invoices (orders, transactions, purchases). Contains total amount, billing address, and date. Use with invoice_line for revenue, spending, and sales analysis.

columns.name: invoice_id
columns.description: The `invoice_id` column represents a unique identifier for each invoice in the database.

columns.name: customer_id
columns.description: The "customer_id" column represents a unique identifier for each customer associated with an invoice.

columns.name: invoice_date
columns.description: The `invoice_date` column represents the date and time when each invoice was issued, recorded as a timestamp without time zone information.
columns.business_rules: Dates range from 2009 to 2013. Fiscal year is Jan-Dec.

columns.name: billing_address
columns.description: The "billing_address" column represents the physical addresses where invoices are sent for billing purposes.
examples: Rua dos Campeões Europeus de Viena, 4350

columns.name: billing_city
columns.description: The `billing_city` column represents the names of cities where customers are billed for their invoices.

columns.name: billing_state
columns.description: The "billing_state" column represents the abbreviated state or province codes associated with the billing address of an invoice.

columns.name: billing_country
columns.description: The "billing_country" column represents the country associated with the billing address for each invoice.

columns.name: billing_postal_code
columns.description: The `billing_postal_code` column represents the postal or ZIP codes associated with billing addresses for invoices, accommodating various formats from different regions.

columns.name: total
columns.description: The "total" column represents the total monetary amount of each invoice, expressed as a numeric value.
columns.business_rules: Sum of (unit_price * quantity) from invoice_line items

tables.name: invoice_line
tables.description: Line items for invoices. Each row is a track purchase with unit_price and quantity. Join to invoice for revenue, sales totals, and spending by customer or region.

columns.name: invoice_line_id
columns.description: The `invoice_line_id` column represents a unique identifier for each line item in an invoice.

columns.name: invoice_id
columns.description: The "invoice_id" column represents a unique identifier for each invoice associated with the corresponding line items in the invoice_line table.

columns.name: track_id
columns.description: The "track_id" column represents a unique identifier for each music track associated with an invoice line item.

columns.name: unit_price
columns.description: The "unit_price" column represents the price per individual unit of a product or service listed in an invoice line, expressed as a numeric value.

columns.name: quantity
columns.description: The "quantity" column represents the number of units of a specific item included in an invoice line.

tables.name: media_type
tables.description: Audio file formats and encoding types (MPEG, AAC, Protected AAC, etc.).

columns.name: media_type_id
columns.description: The `media_type_id` column represents a unique identifier for different types of media in the database.

columns.name: name
columns.description: The "name" column represents the various types of media files, specifying their format and any associated attributes such as protection or purchase status.

tables.name: playlist
tables.description: User-created playlists (collections, mixtapes) grouping tracks together.

columns.name: playlist_id
columns.description: The `playlist_id` column represents a unique identifier for each playlist in the database.

columns.name: name
columns.description: The "name" column represents the titles or categories of playlists, indicating the type of content they contain, such as TV shows, movies, or music.

tables.name: playlist_track
tables.description: Junction table linking playlists to tracks. Many-to-many: a track can appear in multiple playlists.

columns.name: playlist_id
columns.description: The `playlist_id` column represents the unique identifier for each playlist to which a track belongs.

columns.name: track_id
columns.description: The column "track_id" represents a unique identifier for each track in the playlist, with values indicating the specific tracks associated with the playlist.

tables.name: track
tables.description: Individual songs (tracks, recordings). Includes name, duration (milliseconds), size (bytes), unit_price, composer. Belongs to an album, genre, and media_type.

columns.name: track_id
columns.description: The column "track_id" represents a unique identifier for each track in the database.

columns.name: name
columns.description: The "name" column represents the titles of music tracks in the database.
examples: I Still Haven't Found What I'm Looking for

columns.name: album_id
columns.description: The album_id column represents the unique identifier for the album to which each track belongs.

columns.name: media_type_id
columns.description: The `media_type_id` column represents the unique identifier for different types of media associated with each track, such as audio files or video files.

columns.name: genre_id
columns.description: The `genre_id` column represents a unique identifier for different music genres associated with each track in the database.

columns.name: composer
columns.description: The "composer" column represents the names of individuals or groups who composed the musical tracks, with multiple composers listed for some entries.
examples: Alex Van Halen/David Lee Roth/Edward Van Halen/Michael Anthony

columns.name: milliseconds
columns.description: The "milliseconds" column represents the duration of each track in milliseconds.

columns.name: bytes
columns.description: The "bytes" column represents the size of each track in bytes.

columns.name: unit_price
columns.description: The "unit_price" column represents the price of a single unit of a track, expressed as a numeric value.

glossary.churn: Customer with no invoice in last 90 days
glossary.active: Customer with at least one invoice in last 90 days
glossary.revenue: Sum of invoice totals (calculated from invoice.total)

additional_descriptions: Fiscal year runs January to December. Q1=Jan-Mar, Q2=Apr-Jun, Q3=Jul-Sep, Q4=Oct-Dec.
'Recent' means last 30 days. 'Last month' means previous calendar month.
'Top' without a number means top 10. 'Top performers' uses revenue ranking.
A customer is 'active' if they have an invoice in the last 90 days.
Sales regions are based on billing_country in the invoice table.
